lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS231N Lecture 2: Image Classification with Linear Classifiers

unit.name: Linear classifiers as parametric models
unit.description: Introduces the linear classifier as a parametric mapping from image vectors to class scores, then interprets its weights visually and geometrically.
unit.summary: This segment transitions from nonparametric nearest neighbor to a parametric approach that learns weights W to map an input image x into class scores [ts=2824s][ts=2827s][ts=2834s][ts=2846s]. For CIFAR-10, the image is flattened into a 3072-dimensional vector and multiplied by a weight matrix W of shape 10 x 3072 to produce 10 class scores, with a bias term b added for input-independent shifts [ts=2884s][ts=2894s][ts=2914s][ts=2918s][ts=2930s]. The instructor emphasizes that such linear layers are core building blocks of larger neural networks [ts=2960s][ts=2968s][ts=2996s]. A toy 2 x 2 image example shows the algebraic computation of class scores [ts=3018s][ts=3042s][ts=3058s]. Two interpretations follow: each row of W can be viewed as a class template that the image is scored against [ts=3105s][ts=3126s][ts=3141s], and geometrically the classifier creates separating lines or hyperplanes, with bias preventing all boundaries from being forced through the origin [ts=3175s][ts=3207s][ts=3222s].

key_points.text: A linear classifier maps an image vector to class scores with f(x,W)=Wx+b.
For CIFAR-10, a 32 x 32 x 3 image becomes a 3072-dimensional input vector.
With 10 classes, W has shape 10 x 3072 and the output score vector has shape 10 x 1.
Rows of W can be interpreted as class templates learned from data.
The bias term lets geometric decision boundaries shift away from the origin.

kp_catalog.name: Linear classifier as a parametric score function
kp_catalog.description: A learned affine map f(x,W)=Wx+b that converts a flattened image into class scores.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because linear classifier as a parametric score function formalizes how a learned affine map f(x,W)=Wx+b that converts a flattened image into class scores, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Visual and geometric interpretation of weights and bias
kp_catalog.description: Rows of W can be viewed as class templates, while geometrically the affine map induces class-separating hyperplanes that are shifted by the bias term.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because visual and geometric interpretation of weights and bias clarifies rows of W can be viewed as class templates, while geometrically the affine map induces class-separating hyperplanes that are shifted by the bias term, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
